findings.title: pytest uses deprecated --setup-show flag
findings.description: The solution uses `pytest --setup-show` which is deprecated in pytest 8.0+. The modern equivalent is `--setup-plan` or use `pytest -v` for verbose fixture setup output.
findings.rationale: pytest 8.0 changed fixture setup output flags

findings.title: Docker base image not version-pinned
findings.description: The solution uses `python:3.13-slim` which will always pull latest 3.13.x. Best practice is to pin to specific patch version like `python:3.13.1-slim` for reproducible builds.
findings.rationale: Pinning patch versions ensures deterministic builds

findings.title: pytest capture flag deprecated syntax
findings.description: Uses `pytest -s` shorthand (equivalent to --capture=no). The long form --capture=no is clearer and more explicit.
findings.rationale: Explicit flags are more readable and less error-prone

findings.title: PostgreSQL version outdated in example
findings.description: The Docker Compose example uses PostgreSQL 15. Current stable is PostgreSQL 17 (released September 2024). The course should reference a more current version.
findings.rationale: PostgreSQL 17 is the current stable version

findings.title: GitLab CI uses deprecated 'only:' keyword
findings.description: The GitLab CI example uses the deprecated `only:` keyword. Modern GitLab CI uses `rules:` for workflow control.

findings.title: Missing comma in JSON array
findings.description: The lesson.json file has a missing comma after one of the content filenames in the contentFiles array.
findings.rationale: Valid JSON requires commas between array elements

findings.title: Challenge ID references wrong module number
findings.description: The challenge.json has id 'module-16-lesson-08-challenge-1' but this is module 17.
findings.rationale: ID should match the actual module number (17)

findings.title: Railway CLI login pattern outdated
findings.description: The example shows `railway login` as a separate step. Modern Railway CLI (v3+) uses browser-based auth automatically on first command.
findings.suggestedFix:
# Login is automatic on first command, or use:
railway login --browserless
findings.rationale: Railway CLI v3 changed authentication flow

findings.lessonTitle: Modern CI Pipeline with GitHub Actions
findings.title: GitHub Actions version reference
findings.description: Uses actions/checkout@v4 which is current, but could mention that v5 may be available or that v4 is the current standard.
findings.suggestedFix: uses: actions/checkout@v4  # Latest stable as of 2025
findings.rationale: Version pinning should note currency status
